title: FFUF VHost and Subdomain Discovery
description: This runbook operationalizes FFUF VHost and Subdomain Discovery using #ffuf . It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments. Detects virtual hosts and hidden subdomains tied to the same target IP.

metadata.descriptionHtml:
This runbook operationalizes FFUF VHost and Subdomain Discovery using #ffuf. It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments.

Detects virtual hosts and hidden subdomains tied to the same target IP.

  1. Prepare scope and access: validate target reachability and select low-noise execution order.
  2. Execute core workflow: run the command chain to produce actionable security outcomes.
  3. Validate and operationalize output: confirm results, capture artifacts, and prepare follow-on actions.
  • Tool Location/Source: primary references are in local source corpus entries linked from runbook-list.md; e.g., docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/n0xturne-OSCP-Cheat-Sheet-2024/README.md:339; docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/hackmd-oscp-pen200.md:47.
  • Attack pre-requisite: authorized scope, operator shell access, and protocol reachability on TCP 80, TCP 443.
  • What attacker gain: validated findings (access paths, credentials, execution context, or exposure evidence) enabling faster compromise progression.
  • Detection/Defense opsec: monitor abnormal auth/enumeration bursts, enforce least privilege and hardening baselines, and alert on repeated tool-pattern telemetry.

Core Commands:

ffuf -w /usr/share/wordlists/seclists/Discovery/DNS/subdomains-top1million-5000.txt:FUZZ -u http://FUZZ.<DOMAIN>/ -ic
ffuf -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/DNS/subdomains-top1million-110000.txt -H "Host: FUZZ.<DOMAIN>" -u http://<DOMAIN> -c -mc 200
extraInfo: primary references are in local source corpus entries linked from runbook-list.md ; e.g., docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/n0xturne-OSCP-Cheat-Sheet-2024/README.md:339; docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/hackmd-oscp-pen200.md:47 .

steps.tooltip: Early validation reduces noise and prevents invalid follow-on actions against unreachable or mismatched targets.

steps.name: Primary Execution
steps.description: Main command path executes against the target service or domain component.
steps.tooltip: The technique leverages service behavior, protocol assumptions, or misconfiguration to extract actionable state.
